question: How should the old {{target}} be replaced by the new {{replacement}}?
plain_language_preface: We're replacing {{target}} with {{replacement}}. The replacement strategy determines whether callers break during transition and how easy rollback is.

options.label: Hard swap — delete old, insert new in one migration
options.tradeoff: Clean; no backwards-compat shim needed. No rollback path.

options.label: Alias — keep old name, point to new implementation
options.tradeoff: Zero caller breakage; alias debt accumulates.

options.label: Parallel run — both old + new active; switch via feature flag
Staged deprecation — mark old deprecated, replace in next stage
options.tradeoff: Gradual; callers get migration window. Requires two-stage plan.

options.label: Skip replace — patch old in place

recommended_rationale: Staged deprecation gives callers a migration window and avoids breaking changes; use hard swap (A) only for internal-only symbols with no external callers.
